# Weblate

> Weblate is copylefted libre software for continuous localization. It is available as a hosted service and as self-hosted software with tight version control integration.

Official website: https://weblate.org/
Documentation: https://docs.weblate.org/
Hosted service: https://hosted.weblate.org/
Source code: https://github.com/WeblateOrg/weblate

Crawling and AI content-use permissions are governed by https://weblate.org/robots.txt and its Content-Signal declaration.

## Product

- [Features](https://weblate.org/features/): Overview of Weblate capabilities for translators, developers, and localization teams.
- [Hosting and pricing](https://weblate.org/hosting/): Hosted Weblate plans, self-hosted support, and service comparison.
- [Download](https://weblate.org/download/): Source code, installation pointers, deployment guides, and related repositories.
- [Support](https://weblate.org/support/): Community support, paid support, consulting, installation, and backup services.
- [Contribute](https://weblate.org/contribute/): Ways to report issues, contribute code, translate Weblate, and improve documentation.

## Documentation

- [Documentation home](https://docs.weblate.org/): Main documentation for Weblate users and administrators.
- [Installation](https://docs.weblate.org/en/latest/admin/install.html): Recommended setup paths for self-hosted Weblate.
- [Docker installation](https://docs.weblate.org/en/latest/admin/install/docker.html): Docker-based deployment guide.
- [Administration](https://docs.weblate.org/en/latest/admin/index.html): Server administration, configuration, maintenance, and operations.
- [User guide](https://docs.weblate.org/en/latest/user/index.html): Translation workflows and user-facing Weblate features.
- [API](https://docs.weblate.org/en/latest/api.html): Weblate REST API reference and integration entry point.
- [File formats](https://docs.weblate.org/en/latest/formats.html): Supported localization file formats.
- [Contributing](https://docs.weblate.org/en/latest/contributing/index.html): Development and documentation contribution guide.

## Community and Source

- [Main repository](https://github.com/WeblateOrg/weblate): Weblate source code, issues, pull requests, and releases.
- [GitHub organization](https://github.com/WeblateOrg): Related Weblate repositories.
- [Issues](https://github.com/WeblateOrg/weblate/issues): Public bug reports and feature requests.
- [Discussions](https://github.com/WeblateOrg/weblate/discussions): Community questions and development discussion.
- [Translate Weblate](https://hosted.weblate.org/engage/weblate/): Hosted Weblate project for translating Weblate itself.

## Optional

- [News](https://weblate.org/news/): Weblate release announcements and project updates.
- [About](https://weblate.org/about/): Weblate history, team, contributors, and contact information.
- [Donate](https://weblate.org/donate/): Donation options for supporting Weblate development.
- [Privacy policy](https://weblate.org/privacy/): Privacy and data processing information.
- [Terms](https://weblate.org/terms/): Service terms.
- [Security reporting](https://weblate.org/.well-known/security.txt): Security contact and reporting metadata.
